compiled_state.title: As a developer, reviewers can escalate uncertain findings to a more capable model for verification
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add ESCALATE_REVIEW signal that any reviewer below opus can emit for findings it is uncertain about, with orchestrator handling to dispatch the next model tier with focused context.
**Why**: Lower-tier models (haiku, sonnet) lack the capability to make fragile-vs-nitpick distinctions reliably but have no mechanism to escalate uncertainty to a more capable model.
**Scope**:
- IN: ESCALATE_REVIEW signal schema definition, reviewer prompt updates (light, standard), opus guard, deep-tier parallel deduplication, orchestrator escalation handling, escalate-first ordering
- OUT: One-way ratchet persistence (Story 4). approach_viability_concern (Story 6).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, light and standard tier reviewers can emit an ESCALATE_REVIEW signal that includes the list of uncertain finding IDs
  ← Satisfies: "Any reviewer below opus can emit an ESCALATE_REVIEW signal for findings it is uncertain about"
- When this story is complete, the orchestrator dispatches the escalated reviewer (haiku→sonnet, sonnet→opus) with focused context containing only the uncertain findings before entering resolution
  ← Satisfies: "the orchestrator re-dispatches those findings to the next model tier with focused context before entering resolution"
- When this story is complete, an ESCALATE_REVIEW signal emitted by opus is explicitly ignored by the orchestrator with a logged warning
  ← Satisfies: "Opus verdicts on escalated findings are terminal"
- When this story is complete, concurrent ESCALATE_REVIEW signals from parallel deep-tier sonnet reviewers are collected, deduplicated, and dispatched as a single opus escalation
  ← Satisfies: "Any reviewer below opus can emit an ESCALATE_REVIEW signal"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Adversarial] reviewer-base.md mandatory output contract enforces exactly 5 lines — ESCALATE_REVIEW must either extend the contract with an optional sixth line or be embedded in an existing field (e.g., appended to findings JSON)
- [Adversarial] Deep-tier parallel flow (3 sonnet → opus synthesis) is strict sequential — ESCALATE_REVIEW from sonnet agents must be intercepted between sonnet completion and opus dispatch. Define the interception point explicitly
- [Adversarial] Resolution sub-agents (sonnet) must NOT emit ESCALATE_REVIEW — the signal is reserved for reviewer agents only. Resolution output contract (review-fix-dispatch.md) must explicitly exclude it
- [Reliability] ESCALATE_REVIEW schema must require the uncertain findings list — without it, escalation produces a full re-review instead of targeted uncertainty resolution

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
